title: Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary
summary: A garden compost boundary focused on runoff, pests, handwashing, and keeping edible harvest handling separate from scraps.
content: Compost can support soil, but careless compost spreads pathogens, pests, smell, and conflict. Keep human waste out of garden compost, control animal access, separate raw scraps from harvest handling, wash hands after touching compost, and keep runoff away from water and kitchens.
materials: Gloves if available. Context: “Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary”.
Cover material. Context: “Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary”.
steps: Choose a place away from water, kitchens, and child play.
Keep feces, diapers, and medical waste out.
Wash hands and tools after handling.
Stop use after flood, sewage, or chemical contamination.
warnings: Do not put feces into food-garden compost without expert sanitation systems.
Do not apply questionable compost to crops eaten raw.
Do not let animals spread compost into living areas.
common_mistakes: Treating every organic waste as safe.
Placing the pile uphill from water.
Handling compost then preparing food without washing.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary.
short_term: For the first hours of Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary to the real environment: check water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
